title: MCP Tool-Call Provenance Envelope
description: JSON Schema for the Tool-Call Provenance Envelope structure as defined in the MCP Provenance Envelope Specification v2 (2026-05-13). Validates top-level envelope fields, signature structure, sources array, and related registry interfaces.

version.description: Profile identifier declaring the envelope version and signature algorithm suite. Format: mcp-provenance/{YYYY-MM-DD}-{algorithm-suite}. Must be a registered profile identifier.

tool_call_id.description: Non-empty ASCII identifier uniquely identifying the originating tool-call request. Generated by the Client with at least 96 bits of cryptographic entropy and copied verbatim by the Producer.

tool_id.description: Decentralized Identifier (DID) conforming to DID-CORE syntax. Must resolve to a DID document containing the public key identified by signature.protected_header.key_id.

tool_version.description: Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 formatted version of the tool that produced the result. Must not be a placeholder like 0.0.0 or dev.

invoked_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) of when the tool began execution. Sub-second precision is optional.

invoked_by.description: Decentralized Identifier (DID) of the invoking agent.

request_digest.description: Digest over the canonical form of the originating tools/call request parameters. Format: {algorithm}:{lowercase-hex}. Algorithm must be sha-256 or a registered value.

result_digest.description: Digest over the canonical form of the tools/call result content (excluding the provenance field). Format: {algorithm}:{lowercase-hex}.

sources.description: Non-empty ordered array of upstream sources. Each element identifies an external data origin that materially contributed to the result. For purely generative results, include a single element with type: 'llm'.

transformations.description: Optional ordered list of post-source transformations applied between retrieval and result.

confidence.description: Optional Producer self-reported confidence in the result, on a scale of 0 to 1. Informative only.

source.description: Upstream data source that materially contributed to the result.

type.description: Type of source. 'uri' for web/network resources, 'llm' for generative model outputs, 'internal' for locally generated or cached data.

url.description: Required for type='uri'. The upstream URL from which data was retrieved.

retrieved_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) when the source was accessed or generated.

hash.description: Optional hash of the retrieved source content, formatted as {algorithm}:{lowercase-hex}.

weight.description: Optional relative weight or confidence of this source within the aggregation.

signature.description: Container for protected_header (included in canonical form and signed) and value (excluded from canonical form).

value.description: Base64url-encoded signature bytes (RFC 4648 section 5, no padding). The actual cryptographic signature over the canonical form of the envelope with signature.value absent.

protected_header.description: Signature algorithm and key identifier. Both fields are included in the canonical form and covered by the signature.

alg.description: Signature algorithm identifier. Must match the algorithm declared in the version profile identifier.

key_id.description: DID URL identifying the specific signing key within the tool_id DID document. Must be resolved from the tool_id DID and must exist in the document after signature verification.

trust_anchor_record.description: Trust anchor record returned by a Conforming Provenance Registry. Asserts that a specific key_id is authoritative for a tool_id during a validity window.

key_id.description: DID URL of the signing key being anchored. Must match signature.protected_header.key_id in the envelope.

tool_id.description: DID of the tool whose key is being anchored.

issued_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) when the trust anchor was issued. Must not be in the future relative to envelope.invoked_at.

expires_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) after which this record is no longer valid. Must not precede envelope.invoked_at.

issuer.description: DID or URI of the registry that issued this trust anchor record.

issuer_signature.description: Base64url-encoded signature over the canonical form of this record with issuer_signature absent, signed by the issuer's key.

revocation_record.description: Revocation record asserting that a specific key_id has been compromised or is no longer authoritative.

key_id.description: DID URL of the revoked signing key.

revoked_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) when the key was revoked. Envelopes produced before this timestamp are not retroactively invalidated unless independently verified.

issuer_signature.description: Base64url-encoded signature over the canonical form of this record with issuer_signature absent, signed by the registry's key.

trust_anchor_feed.description: Signed feed of trust anchor and revocation records from a Conforming Provenance Registry.

generated_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) when the feed was generated.

expires_at.description: UTC timestamp (RFC 3339) after which the feed expires. Interval from generated_at to expires_at must not exceed 24 hours.

revocations.description: Ordered array of revocation_record objects issued within the past 90 days.

merkle_root.description: SHA-256 Merkle root of all trust_anchor_records and revocation_records, formatted as sha256:<lowercase-hex>.

feed_signature.description: Base64url-encoded signature over the canonical form of this feed with feed_signature absent, signed by the registry's key.

registry_lookup_request.description: Request to a Conforming Provenance Registry to look up a trust anchor record.

key_id.description: DID URL of the signing key.

found.description: True if a trust anchor record was found for the tool_id and key_id pair.

record.description: Trust anchor record when found is true; absent when false.

revocation.description: Revocation record if the key is revoked; absent otherwise.

feed_root.description: SHA-256 Merkle root of the trust-anchor feed at time of response, formatted as sha256:<lowercase-hex>.

registry_id.description: DID or URI identifier of the registry, used by Consumers to apply multi-issuer policy.

registry_get_feed_request.description: Request to a registry to fetch the current signed trust-anchor feed.

registry_get_feed_response.description: Metadata for fetching the trust-anchor feed from a registry.

feed_url.description: URL of the signed trust-anchor feed.

registry_key_jwk.description: Registry's public key in JWK format, used to verify feed_signature and issuer_signature fields.
